top_stories.title: DBCC opposes suspension of excise tax on gas, diesel
top_stories.summary: SUSPENDING EXCISE TAXES on diesel and gasoline would only provide limited relief compared to lifting levies on liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) and kerosene as the resulting decline in pump prices would be small, the Department of Finance said.
top_stories.why_it_matters: This could change the rules or incentives around how AI companies build and ship products. It has consequences beyond a single product cycle.

top_stories.title: ₱510M losses a month seen on tax pause
top_stories.summary: THE suspension of excise taxes imposed on kerosene and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) will result in a revenue loss of P510 million per month for the Bureau of Customs (BOC).

top_stories.title: Philippines seeks US nod to extend Russian oil imports amid supply risks
top_stories.summary: The Philippines is seeking an extension of a United States sanctions waiver to allow continued oil imports from Russia, as the government moves to secure fuel supply amid ongoing global disruptions, the Department of Energy said.

top_stories.title: NGCP: Middle East war won’t affect electricity grid charges
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — The National Grid Corporation of the Philippines (NGCP) said Wednesday that the Middle East war would have no spillover effect on electricity transmission charges. top_stories.title: Local ride-hailing platforms reduce commission rates
top_stories.summary: Ride-hailing platforms have begun slashing their commission rates in response to the ongoing fuel price crisis, their representatives told a congressional hearing on Monday, as lawmakers pushed for mandatory cuts to protect the earnings of transport network vehicle service (TNVS) and motorcycle taxi drivers.

top_stories.title: Philippines may allow more pork, corn imports at lower tariffs
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines – The Department of Agriculture (DA) is considering to use the minimum access volume (MAV) Plus mechanism to allow increased importation of corn and pork at lower duties. secondary_signals.title: SBMA to reduce tariff, cargo charges by 5% amid Middle East conflict
secondary_signals.summary: The Subic Bay Metropolitan Authority will implement a 5% reduction in tariff, cargo charges and other fees in the Port of Subic as temporary cost-stabilizing measures amid geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

secondary_signals.title: Price Tracker: Oil, fuel monitor for Apr. 15
secondary_signals.summary: The rollback in oil and fuel prices that took effect Tuesday, April 15, only partly offsets the sustained run-up in recent weeks, officials said.

secondary_signals.title: Go justifies limited tax suspension, says move was ‘balanced’
secondary_signals.summary: MANILA, Philippines – Finance Secretary Frederick Go justified the Marcos administration’s decision to avoid a blanket suspension of fuel excise taxes, saying the move was “balanced and fiscally responsible.” In a statement on Tuesday, Go said the Development Budget Coordination Committee (DBCC) recommended limiting...

secondary_signals.title: DBM: Nothing ‘irregular’ with fuel subsidy allocations
secondary_signals.summary: The Department of Budget and Management dismissed speculations of irregularity in the budget for fuel subsidies.

secondary_signals.title: Fuel price rolled back by as much as P23/L
secondary_signals.summary: Fuel price rollbacks by as much as P23 per liter take effect on Tuesday—a rare break from five weeks of surging hikes. secondary_signals.title: Nomura sees 60% chance of BSP rate hikes
secondary_signals.summary: MANILA, Philippines – There is a 60-percent chance that the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) will raise interest rates this year as surging oil prices tied to the conflict in the Middle East fuel inflation, Nomura said, warning that the Philippines could face one of the region’s sharpest stagflationary shocks.
